summary.overall_regression: Critical regression: health score 30 → 40 (-10 pts). 2 critical incident(s).
summary.causal_chain_text: Isolated anomalous events (no confirmed causal chain): 'log file sync' (12.7% DB time) | 'buffer busy waits' (6.5% DB time) | 'DB CPU' (4.5% DB time)
summary.headline: DB Time rose 89% driven by: bottleneck shift I/O→Concurrency + 26 new application SQL(s) (top: fzqdq3nxzjnz9, 940107s) + execution rate surge +314%.
headline_evidence: Top worsened wait: 'log file sync' 0.1%→12.7% DB Time

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.
pathology_investigate: DBWR I/O throughput (db file parallel write avg wait)
heavy DML workload generating dirty buffers

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
pathology_investigate: top segments by buffer busy waits

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Format Block enqueue contention — sessions waiting to format new blocks in the buffer cache. Related to free buffer pressure: when DBWR can't free buffers fast enough, sessions contend on this enqueue while trying to allocate buffer space.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Format Block enqueue contention — sessions waiting to format new blocks in the buffer cache. Related to free buffer pressure: when DBWR can't free buffers fast enough, sessions contend on this enqueue while trying to allocate buffer space.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
pathology_investigate: missing indexes causing unselective index scans

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.
pathology_investigate: undo tablespace usage and free space

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
pathology_investigate: large result sets in top SQL (rows/exec)

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Session waiting for free space in the log buffer. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough, or log_buffer is too small for the redo generation rate.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Session waiting for free space in the log buffer. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough, or log_buffer is too small for the redo generation rate.
pathology_investigate: LGWR write latency (log file parallel write)

comparisons.root_cause_hint: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.
pathology_investigate: which table/index is rapidly growing (DBA_SEGMENTS + AWR segment stats)

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Generic TX enqueue contention — encompasses row lock and index split paths.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Generic TX enqueue contention — encompasses row lock and index split paths.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
pathology_investigate: large result sets in top SQL (rows/exec)

sql_regressions.sql_text_truncated: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_export_orders'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume
sql_regressions.sql_text_full: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_export_orders'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume NUMBER; v_err PLS_INTEGER; v_prepaiddc VARCHAR2(50); BEGIN :RC := 0; pkg_log.info('START '||c_job_name); v_start := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; --Get PREPAID_DC virtual location from parameter SELECT param_value INTO v_prepaiddc FROM u_system_param WHERE param_name='PREPAID_DC'; INSERT INTO igpmgr.udt_poout( loadid, arrivdate, item, qty, loadlineid, source, dest, shipdate, u_supply_planner, pmg_po_notes) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vl.loadid, TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), vll.item, vll.qty, vll.loadlineid, vll .source, vll.dest, TRUNC(vl.shipdate), i.u_supply_planner, o.notes FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.orderheader o, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 AND vl.orderid = o.orderid --08/23/2022 AND vll.source != v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Order Lines exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); --08/23/2022 --NEW logic to convert the PREPAID DC POs to Recship --setting U_PROCESSED TO NULL for these Recships so they are picked up by PMM INSERT INTO scpomgr.recship( source, dest, item, qty, orderplacedate, schedshipdate, schedarrivdate, seqnum, type, U_PROCESSED ) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vll.source, vll.dest, vll.item, vll.qty, TRUNC(SYSDATE), TRUNC(vl.shipdate), TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), '1' as seqnum, '1' as type , NULL as U_PROCESSED FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 --08/23/2022 AND vll.source = v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Pre-Paid Inv Recship exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); UPDATE scpomgr.vehicleload vl SET exported=1 WHERE exported=0 AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM igpmgr.udt_poout i WHERE i.loadid = vl.loadid); pkg_log.info('Orders Marked as Exported: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); COMMIT; v_end := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; v_min := FLOOR(((v_end - v_start) / 100) / 60); v_sec := MOD(((v_end - v_start) / 100), 60); pkg_log.info('TIME TAKEN TO LOAD EXPORT_ORDERS:'||v_min||' MINS '||v_sec||' SECS'); EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN pkg_job.fatal_error; :RC := '1'; ROLLBACK; v_errdesc := SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000); v_errcode := TO_CHAR(SQLCODE); pkg_log.error(c_job_name||' ORACLE ERROR -'||v_errco de||' '||v_errdesc); END;

recommendations.finding: Hot block waits (buffer busy waits) total 6.5% DB time (was 0.6%). Indicates hot index/table blocks — monotonic keys or block-level row clustering.
recommendations.action: Check V$SEGSTAT / DBA_HIST_SEG_STAT for segments with high buffer_busy_waits. Consider reverse-key indexes for monotonic columns. Use DBMS_SPACE.OBJECT_SPACE_USAGE to find hot objects.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: 'log buffer space' waits = 0.70% DB time. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough — sessions stall writing redo entries.
recommendations.action: Increase LOG_BUFFER init parameter (default 3–8 MB; try 32–64 MB for write-heavy workloads). Check redo log storage latency. Ensure redo logs are on separate fast storage.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: Undo-related contention detected (1.2% DB time).  Long readers against changing data risk ORA-01555 snapshot too old errors.
recommendations.action: Check UNDO_RETENTION vs query duration. Run: SELECT name, value FROM v$parameter WHERE name LIKE 'undo%'. Review DBA_UNDO_EXTENTS for shrinkage under pressure.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: Session count surged from 0 to 0 (0 sessions). Logon rate increased 5100%. Application retry loops, pool leaks, or traffic surge likely.
recommendations.action: Check V$SESSION for session sources (PROGRAM, MACHINE, USERNAME). Review application connection pool settings and retry logic. Consider DRCP (Database Resident Connection Pooling) for short-lived connections.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: 'free buffer waits' is a 3.2σ statistical outlier: 58.9% of DB time (baseline mean: 7.2%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
recommendations.action: Server process cannot find a free buffer in the buffer cache. DBWR is posted to write dirty buffers to disk but cannot keep up. Causes cascading contention — other sessions also queue for free buffers.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'log file sync' classified as worsening: 857.2s -> 150100.0s (+17232%) [Both volume and latency increased]
recommendations.action: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'log file sync';
recommendations.impact: 12.7% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'buffer busy waits' classified as worsening: 3895.7s -> 77000.0s (+1856%) [Volume increase — stable latency, more waits]
recommendations.action: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'buffer busy waits';
recommendations.impact: 6.5% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'DB CPU' classified as worsening: 15300.0s -> 53400.0s (+245%) [Volume increase — stable latency, more waits]
recommendations.action: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'DB CPU';
recommendations.impact: 4.5% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'enq: US - contention' classified as new_bottleneck: 0.0s -> 13700.0s (+100%)
recommendations.action: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'enq: US - contention';
recommendations.impact: 1.2% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'SQL*Net more data from client' classified as new_bottleneck: 0.0s -> 13300.0s (+100%)
recommendations.action: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'SQL*Net more data from client';
recommendations.impact: 1.1% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'enq: TX - index contention' classified as new_bottleneck: 0.0s -> 7726.2s (+100%)
recommendations.action: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

sql_maintenance.net_assessment_detail: SQL not present in bad period

all_metrics.label: DB Time/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Total database work per second. Core workload intensity metric.

all_metrics.label: DB CPU/sec
all_metrics.explanation: CPU consumed by database per second. Increasing means more compute demand.

all_metrics.label: Logical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer gets per second. High values indicate intensive SQL scanning.

all_metrics.label: Physical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk read blocks per second. High value with low Read IO Requests/s = large multi-block full-table scans (direct-path, invisible to Segments section). High value with proportionally high Read IO Requests/s = many single-block index reads. Compare both metrics together.

all_metrics.label: Physical Writes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk writes per second. Increase may indicate more DML or redo/undo activity.

all_metrics.label: Redo Size/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Bytes of redo generated per second. Higher means more DML workload.

all_metrics.label: Hard Parses/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Hard parses per second. >100/s indicates literal SQL or cursor invalidation.

all_metrics.label: Executes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions per second. Application throughput indicator.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User commits+rollbacks per second. Primary business throughput KPI.

all_metrics.label: Logons/sec
all_metrics.explanation: New session creations per second. Spike signals connection pool churn.

all_metrics.label: User Calls/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User-originated calls per second. Overall application request rate.

all_metrics.label: Block Changes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer blocks modified per second. Correlates with redo and DML volume.

all_metrics.label: Buffer Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Data found in RAM vs disk. <95% = excessive physical I/O.

all_metrics.label: Library Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL look-ups that found the cursor in memory. Low = shared pool too small.

all_metrics.label: Soft Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions that reused a cached plan. <95% = hard parse storm.

all_metrics.label: Execute to Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: % executes reusing open cursor. Low = cursor caching not enabled.

all_metrics.label: Latch Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Internal Oracle lock efficiency. <99% = contention in shared memory.

all_metrics.label: DB Time (min)
all_metrics.explanation: Total database active time. Core load indicator.

all_metrics.label: Avg Active Sessions
all_metrics.explanation: Sessions actively working. >CPU count = saturation.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Business throughput KPI (commits+rollbacks per second).

all_metrics.label: Elapsed Window
all_metrics.explanation: Observation window length. Large difference = unequal comparison.

all_metrics.label: log file sync
all_metrics.explanation: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.

all_metrics.label: buffer busy waits
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.

all_metrics.label: DB CPU
all_metrics.explanation: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.

all_metrics.label: enq: US - contention
all_metrics.explanation: Undo Segment contention — sessions waiting for undo segment resources. Can indicate undo tablespace is too small or too many concurrent transactions.

all_metrics.label: SQL*Net more data from client
all_metrics.explanation: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.

all_metrics.label: enq: TX - index contention
all_metrics.explanation: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

all_metrics.label: log buffer space
all_metrics.explanation: Session waiting for free space in the log buffer. LGWR cannot flush redo fast enough, or log_buffer is too small for the redo generation rate.

recommendations.finding: Log file sync average wait is 124.9ms, exceeding the 10ms threshold.  Commit latency is elevated.
recommendations.action: Move redo logs to faster storage or reduce commit frequency
recommendations.oracle_fix:
-- Move redo logs to SSD/flash
-- Consider COMMIT WRITE BATCH NOWAIT for non-critical transactions
recommendations.impact: Reduces commit latency and session queuing

recommendations.finding: CPU saturation detected: AAS=651.1 exceeds CPU count 36 x 1.5.
recommendations.action: Identify top CPU SQL, reduce parallelism, or scale CPU
recommendations.oracle_fix:
-- Identify top CPU consumers:
SELECT sql_id, cpu_time/1e6 cpu_s FROM v$sqlstats ORDER BY cpu_time DESC FETCH FIRST 10 ROWS ONLY;
recommendations.impact: Reduces response time and prevents runaway queueing

insights.title: Incident: Critical Sql Disappeared
insights.summary: 4 critical SQL statement(s) present in the GOOD period are completely absent from the BAD period AWR (top: bm5q4vbp3tuvv, c2570kh0gz6sr, 4m2ks9ca4r38q). These accounted for 606978s of elapsed time in the GOOD period. This is strong evidence the batch job or process did NOT complete its primary processing work — it stalled, failed, or was blocked before reaching these execution stages. The lower DB Time in the BAD period reflects less productive work, NOT lower load.

insights.title: ⚠ Baseline Already Stressed — Not a Clean Healthy Reference
insights.summary: The baseline period itself was near-saturated (AAS=348.9 vs 36 CPUs = 969% utilization). The problem period degradation is ON TOP OF an already overloaded system. Performance deltas may understate the true severity.
insights.root_cause: The 'good' period was not a clean healthy baseline — it was already at or near capacity. Issues visible in the problem period may have pre-existing roots.
insights.action: Compare against a genuinely idle/healthy period if available. Look at ADDM findings for the baseline period — any findings indicate pre-existing issues. Do not attribute all problem-period issues solely to new workload.

insights.title: Wait Profile Shifted — User I/O decreased

insights.root_cause: Wait class 'User I/O' changed by -30.4 percentage points.
insights.action: Cross-reference this with the SQL analysis below to find the specific queries driving this shift.

analysis_notes: RULE: ADDM recommendations are guides, not gospel. Validate each recommendation against multiple AWR periods before making system changes. Do NOT increase SGA/PGA based on a single AWR report alone — gather at least 3–5 representative periods.
RULE: AWR averages hide spikes. A 60-minute snapshot can mask a 5-minute crisis. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports or use ASH.
RULE: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio is not a primary health signal. A 99% hit ratio can coexist with a burning system. Investigate wait events and per-execution SQL metrics instead.

investigation_trail.finding: AAS = 348.9 vs 36 CPUs — database severely overloaded, OS CPU busy: 27%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Database is doing 2x more work than CPU capacity. Every other metric matters.

investigation_trail.finding: Snapshot window is 30 minutes — averages hide spikes.
investigation_trail.conclusion: AWR averages can mask a 5-minute crisis inside a 60-minute window. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports (awrrpt.sql) or use ASH (DBA_HIST_ACTIVE_SESS_HISTORY) to isolate the exact problem period.

investigation_trail.finding: #1 non-idle wait: 'free buffer waits' at 55.3% DB time — this is the symptom.
investigation_trail.conclusion: Primary bottleneck category: CONFIGURATION. Next: find Top SQL by Elapsed that links to this wait, then check Segment Statistics to confirm which object is under pressure.

investigation_trail.finding: Top SQL bm5q4vbp3tuvv at 56% DB time — SQL bm5q4vbp3tuvv: 214,654 execs at 1.64s/exec, 4,571 gets/exec.
investigation_trail.conclusion: RULE: Always evaluate per-execution cost, not just totals. High total elapsed from many cheap calls = volume problem. High per-exec cost from few calls = broken query. Check execution plans for full-table scans, missing indexes, and bind variable usage.

investigation_trail.finding: Buffer Hit 92.2%, Soft Parse 99.9%, Library Hit 100.0%, Exec to Parse 92.3%, Latch Hit 99.5%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Efficiency issues detected — see findings.

sql_stats.sql_text: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_export_orders'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume NUMBER; v_err PLS_INTEGER; v_prepaiddc VARCHAR2(50); BEGIN :RC := 0; pkg_log.info('START '||c_job_name); v_start := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; --Get PREPAID_DC virtual location from parameter SELECT param_value INTO v_prepaiddc FROM u_system_param WHERE param_name='PREPAID_DC'; INSERT INTO igpmgr.udt_poout( loadid, arrivdate, item, qty, loadlineid, source, dest, shipdate, u_supply_planner, pmg_po_notes) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vl.loadid, TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), vll.item, vll.qty, vll.loadlineid, vll .source, vll.dest, TRUNC(vl.shipdate), i.u_supply_planner, o.notes FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.orderheader o, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 AND vl.orderid = o.orderid --08/23/2022 AND vll.source != v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Order Lines exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); --08/23/2022 --NEW logic to convert the PREPAID DC POs to Recship --setting U_PROCESSED TO NULL for these Recships so they are picked up by PMM INSERT INTO scpomgr.recship( source, dest, item, qty, orderplacedate, schedshipdate, schedarrivdate, seqnum, type, U_PROCESSED ) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vll.source, vll.dest, vll.item, vll.qty, TRUNC(SYSDATE), TRUNC(vl.shipdate), TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), '1' as seqnum, '1' as type , NULL as U_PROCESSED FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 --08/23/2022 AND vll.source = v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Pre-Paid Inv Recship exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); UPDATE scpomgr.vehicleload vl SET exported=1 WHERE exported=0 AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM igpmgr.udt_poout i WHERE i.loadid = vl.loadid); pkg_log.info('Orders Marked as Exported: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); COMMIT; v_end := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; v_min := FLOOR(((v_end - v_start) / 100) / 60); v_sec := MOD(((v_end - v_start) / 100), 60); pkg_log.info('TIME TAKEN TO LOAD EXPORT_ORDERS:'||v_min||' MINS '||v_sec||' SECS'); EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN pkg_job.fatal_error; :RC := '1'; ROLLBACK; v_errdesc := SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000); v_errcode := TO_CHAR(SQLCODE); pkg_log.error(c_job_name||' ORACLE ERROR -'||v_errco de||' '||v_errdesc); END;

12g4hqvh3dkkq.fullText: INSERT INTO SCPOMGR.RECSHIP( SOURCE, DEST, ITEM, QTY, ORDERPLACEDATE, SCHEDSHIPDATE, SCHEDARRIVDATE, SEQNUM, TYPE, U_PROCESSED ) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ VLL.SOURCE, VLL.DEST, VLL.ITEM, VLL.QTY, TRUNC(SYSDATE), TRUNC(VL.SHIPDATE), TRUNC(VL.ARRIVDATE), '1' AS SEQNUM, '1' AS TYPE , NULL AS U_PROCESSED FROM SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOAD VL, SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOADLINE VLL, SCPOMGR.ITEM I WHERE VL.APPROVALSTATUS=1 AND VL.EXPORTED=0 AND VLL.SOURCE = :B1 AND VL.LOADID = VLL.LOADID AND VLL.ITEM = I.ITEM

1f66gjjbxmrh3.fullText: select case when max_bytes > total_bytes then round((100*(free_bytes+(max_bytes-total_bytes))/max_bytes), 2) else round((100*free_bytes / total_bytes), 2) end as freepercent from (select tablespace_name, max_bytes, total_bytes, nvl(free_bytes, 0) as free_bytes from (select tablespace_name, sum(nvl(maxbytes, 0)) as max_bytes, sum(nvl(bytes, 0)) as total_bytes from (select tablespace_name, case when maxbytes = 0 then bytes else maxbytes end as maxbytes, bytes from dba_data_files) group by tablespace_name), (select tablespace_name fs_ts_name, sum(nvl(bytes, 0)) free_bytes from dba_free_space group by tablespace_name) where tablespace_name = fs_ts_name (+) and tablespace_name='SCPODATA' order by tablespace_name )

3dbzmtf9ahvzt.fullText: merge /* KSXM:OPTIM_DML_INF */ into sys.mon_mods_all$ m using dual on (m.obj# = :objn and (:pobjn = 0 or m.obj# = :pobjn or m.obj# in (select obj# from tabpart$ where bo# = :pobjn and obj# = :objn union all select obj# from tabcompart$ where bo# = :pobjn and o bj# = :objn union all select tsp.obj# from tabsubpart$ tsp, tabcompart$ tcp where tsp.pobj# = tcp.obj# and (tcp.bo# = :pobjn or tcp.obj# = :pobjn) and tsp.obj# = :objn))) when matched then update set inserts = decode(:reset, 1, 0, inserts) + :ins, updates = decode(:reset, 1, 0, updates) + :upd, deletes = decode(:reset, 1, 0, deletes) + :del, flags = decode(:reset, 1, 0, flags - bitand(flags, :flag)) + :flag, drop_segments = decode(:reset, 1, 0, d rop_segments) + :dropseg, timestamp = :time when NOT matched then insert (obj#, inserts, updates, deletes, timestamp, flags, drop_segments) values (:objn, :ins, :upd, :del, :time, :flag, :dropseg) where :ins != 0 or :upd != 0 or :del != 0 or :flag != 0 or :dropseg !=0

3k0m8427v2r8r.fullText: UPDATE SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOAD VL SET EXPORTED=1 WHERE EXPORTED=0 AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM IGPMGR.UDT_POOUT I WHERE I.LOADID = VL.LOADID)

4qguf81v4r5mx.fullText: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B3 WHERE NODE_STATE = :B2 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B1 RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0

4tf42bahkszhd.fullText: UPDATE SRE_ACTION SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , LAST_UPDATE_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC (SYSTIMESTAMP) WHERE LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL AND ACTION_GROUP_SET_ID = :B3 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND STATE <> :B2 AND GROUP_NUMBER = :B1 RETURNING ACTION_NUMBER, GROUP_NUMBER, NAME, STATE, RETRY_COUNT INTO :O0 , :O1 , :O2 , :O3 , :O4

fnfqakfjjmd67.fullText: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_export_orders'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume NUMBER; v_err PLS_INTEGER; v_prepaiddc VARCHAR2(50); BEGIN :RC := 0; pkg_log.info('START '||c_job_name); v_start := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; --Get PREPAID_DC virtual location from parameter SELECT param_value INTO v_prepaiddc FROM u_system_param WHERE param_name='PREPAID_DC'; INSERT INTO igpmgr.udt_poout( loadid, arrivdate, item, qty, loadlineid, source, dest, shipdate, u_supply_planner, pmg_po_notes) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vl.loadid, TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), vll.item, vll.qty, vll.loadlineid, vll .source, vll.dest, TRUNC(vl.shipdate), i.u_supply_planner, o.notes FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.orderheader o, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 AND vl.orderid = o.orderid --08/23/2022 AND vll.source != v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Order Lines exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); --08/23/2022 --NEW logic to convert the PREPAID DC POs to Recship --setting U_PROCESSED TO NULL for these Recships so they are picked up by PMM INSERT INTO scpomgr.recship( source, dest, item, qty, orderplacedate, schedshipdate, schedarrivdate, seqnum, type, U_PROCESSED ) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vll.source, vll.dest, vll.item, vll.qty, TRUNC(SYSDATE), TRUNC(vl.shipdate), TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), '1' as seqnum, '1' as type , NULL as U_PROCESSED FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 --08/23/2022 AND vll.source = v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Pre-Paid Inv Recship exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); UPDATE scpomgr.vehicleload vl SET exported=1 WHERE exported=0 AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM igpmgr.udt_poout i WHERE i.loadid = vl.loadid); pkg_log.info('Orders Marked as Exported: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); COMMIT; v_end := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; v_min := FLOOR(((v_end - v_start) / 100) / 60); v_sec := MOD(((v_end - v_start) / 100), 60); pkg_log.info('TIME TAKEN TO LOAD EXPORT_ORDERS:'||v_min||' MINS '||v_sec||' SECS'); EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN pkg_job.fatal_error; :RC := '1'; ROLLBACK; v_errdesc := SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000); v_errcode := TO_CHAR(SQLCODE); pkg_log.error(c_job_name||' ORACLE ERROR -'||v_errco de||' '||v_errdesc); END;

_sql_text_map.12g4hqvh3dkkq: INSERT INTO SCPOMGR.RECSHIP( SOURCE, DEST, ITEM, QTY, ORDERPLACEDATE, SCHEDSHIPDATE, SCHEDARRIVDATE, SEQNUM, TYPE, U_PROCESSED ) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ VLL.SOURCE, VLL.DEST, VLL.ITEM, VLL.QTY, TRUNC(SYSDATE), TRUNC(VL.SHIPDATE), TRUNC(VL.ARRIVDATE), '1' AS SEQNUM, '1' AS TYPE , NULL AS U_PROCESSED FROM SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOAD VL, SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOADLINE VLL, SCPOMGR.ITEM I WHERE VL.APPROVALSTATUS=1 AND VL.EXPORTED=0 AND VLL.SOURCE = :B1 AND VL.LOADID = VLL.LOADID AND VLL.ITEM = I.ITEM

_sql_text_map.1f66gjjbxmrh3: select case when max_bytes > total_bytes then round((100*(free_bytes+(max_bytes-total_bytes))/max_bytes), 2) else round((100*free_bytes / total_bytes), 2) end as freepercent from (select tablespace_name, max_bytes, total_bytes, nvl(free_bytes, 0) as free_bytes from (select tablespace_name, sum(nvl(maxbytes, 0)) as max_bytes, sum(nvl(bytes, 0)) as total_bytes from (select tablespace_name, case when maxbytes = 0 then bytes else maxbytes end as maxbytes, bytes from dba_data_files) group by tablespace_name), (select tablespace_name fs_ts_name, sum(nvl(bytes, 0)) free_bytes from dba_free_space group by tablespace_name) where tablespace_name = fs_ts_name (+) and tablespace_name='SCPODATA' order by tablespace_name )
_sql_text_map.3dbzmtf9ahvzt: merge /* KSXM:OPTIM_DML_INF */ into sys.mon_mods_all$ m using dual on (m.obj# = :objn and (:pobjn = 0 or m.obj# = :pobjn or m.obj# in (select obj# from tabpart$ where bo# = :pobjn and obj# = :objn union all select obj# from tabcompart$ where bo# = :pobjn and o bj# = :objn union all select tsp.obj# from tabsubpart$ tsp, tabcompart$ tcp where tsp.pobj# = tcp.obj# and (tcp.bo# = :pobjn or tcp.obj# = :pobjn) and tsp.obj# = :objn))) when matched then update set inserts = decode(:reset, 1, 0, inserts) + :ins, updates = decode(:reset, 1, 0, updates) + :upd, deletes = decode(:reset, 1, 0, deletes) + :del, flags = decode(:reset, 1, 0, flags - bitand(flags, :flag)) + :flag, drop_segments = decode(:reset, 1, 0, d rop_segments) + :dropseg, timestamp = :time when NOT matched then insert (obj#, inserts, updates, deletes, timestamp, flags, drop_segments) values (:objn, :ins, :upd, :del, :time, :flag, :dropseg) where :ins != 0 or :upd != 0 or :del != 0 or :flag != 0 or :dropseg !=0
_sql_text_map.3k0m8427v2r8r: UPDATE SCPOMGR.VEHICLELOAD VL SET EXPORTED=1 WHERE EXPORTED=0 AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM IGPMGR.UDT_POOUT I WHERE I.LOADID = VL.LOADID)

_sql_text_map.dtknc5b5g6h0h: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B1 WHERE (((NODE_STATE = :B5 ) AND (SRE_NODE.STOP_REQUEST_DATE < (SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - :B4 )) ) OR (NODE_STATE = :B3 ) ) AND (CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B2 AND LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL AND NODE_ID <> :B1 AND ROWNUM = 1) RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0
_sql_text_map.fnfqakfjjmd67: DECLARE c_job_name CONSTANT VARCHAR2(50) :='ff_export_orders'; v_errcode VARCHAR2(50); v_errdesc VARCHAR2(1000); v_start PLS_INTEGER; v_end PLS_INTEGER; v_min PLS_INTEGER; v_sec PLS_INTEGER; v_volume NUMBER; v_err PLS_INTEGER; v_prepaiddc VARCHAR2(50); BEGIN :RC := 0; pkg_log.info('START '||c_job_name); v_start := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; --Get PREPAID_DC virtual location from parameter SELECT param_value INTO v_prepaiddc FROM u_system_param WHERE param_name='PREPAID_DC'; INSERT INTO igpmgr.udt_poout( loadid, arrivdate, item, qty, loadlineid, source, dest, shipdate, u_supply_planner, pmg_po_notes) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vl.loadid, TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), vll.item, vll.qty, vll.loadlineid, vll .source, vll.dest, TRUNC(vl.shipdate), i.u_supply_planner, o.notes FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.orderheader o, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 AND vl.orderid = o.orderid --08/23/2022 AND vll.source != v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Order Lines exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); --08/23/2022 --NEW logic to convert the PREPAID DC POs to Recship --setting U_PROCESSED TO NULL for these Recships so they are picked up by PMM INSERT INTO scpomgr.recship( source, dest, item, qty, orderplacedate, schedshipdate, schedarrivdate, seqnum, type, U_PROCESSED ) SELECT /*+ parallel() */ vll.source, vll.dest, vll.item, vll.qty, TRUNC(SYSDATE), TRUNC(vl.shipdate), TRUNC(vl.arrivdate), '1' as seqnum, '1' as type , NULL as U_PROCESSED FROM scpomgr.vehicleload vl, scpomgr.vehicleloadline vll, scpomgr.item i WHERE vl.approvalstatus=1 AND vl.exported=0 --08/23/2022 AND vll.source = v_prepaiddc AND vl.loadid = vll.loadid AND vll.item = i.item; pkg_log.info('Pre-Paid Inv Recship exported to PMM: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); UPDATE scpomgr.vehicleload vl SET exported=1 WHERE exported=0 AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM igpmgr.udt_poout i WHERE i.loadid = vl.loadid); pkg_log.info('Orders Marked as Exported: '||SQL%ROWCOUNT); COMMIT; v_end := DBMS_UTILITY.GET_TIME; v_min := FLOOR(((v_end - v_start) / 100) / 60); v_sec := MOD(((v_end - v_start) / 100), 60); pkg_log.info('TIME TAKEN TO LOAD EXPORT_ORDERS:'||v_min||' MINS '||v_sec||' SECS'); EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN pkg_job.fatal_error; :RC := '1'; ROLLBACK; v_errdesc := SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000); v_errcode := TO_CHAR(SQLCODE); pkg_log.error(c_job_name||' ORACLE ERROR -'||v_errco de||' '||v_errdesc); END;

2h7u1gwu558r7.fullText: INSERT INTO PROCESSSKU ( PROCESSID, ITEM, LOC, ITEMLEVEL, SKULEVEL, NETCHGSW, BATCHNUM ) VALUES ( :B4 , :B1 , :B2 , 0, 0, 1, :B3 )

2qjq3d3u54ajq.fullText: SELECT 1 FROM GV$SESSION A WHERE SID = :B3 AND SERIAL# = :B2 AND INST_ID = :B1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM MANUGISTICS_SCHEMA B WHERE A.USERNAME = B.SCHEMA_NAME AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYS' AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME <> 'JDA_SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME IS NOT NULL)

4pp47nzdd67qr.fullText: SELECT /* SafetyStockMaxPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC

4qguf81v4r5mx.fullText: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B3 WHERE NODE_STATE = :B2 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B1 RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0

5dqz0hqtp9fru.fullText: select /*+ connect_by_filtering index(sysauth$ i_sysauth1) */ privilege#, bitand(nvl(option$, 0), 72), grantee#, level from sysauth$ connect by grantee#=prior privilege# and privilege#>0 start with grantee#=:1 and privilege#>0

6jb71hzd26m1u.fullText: SELECT /* SafetyStockPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc, sspresentation.displayqty, sspresentation.dmdgroup, sspresentation.presentationqty FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC

7j17vjtnkzn98.fullText: SELECT /* TimePhasedProfileRetrievalHint */ DISTINCT spp.stockoutlvl as stockOutLevel, spp.item as item, spp.loc as loc FROM processsku, SKUPlanningParam spp WHERE processsku.item = spp.item and processsku.loc = spp.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3

865qwpcdyggkk.fullText: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1

9huj1akp5b757.fullText: SELECT /*+ ORDERED USE_NL(PROCESSSKU SKU) INDEX (PROCESSSKU PROCESSSKU_BATCH) INDEX (SKU SKU_PK) INDEX (LOC LOC_PK) */ /* SKUBatchRetrievalHint */ sku.item item, sku.loc loc, replentype , (case when sku.ohpost > to_date('01/02/1970', 'mm/dd/yyyy') then sku.ohpost else loc.ohpost end) trueohpost FROM processsku, SKU sku, LOC loc WHERE sku.item = processsku.item and sku.loc = processsku.loc AND processsku.loc = loc.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2

9zg9qd9bm4spu.fullText: update user$ set spare6=DECODE(to_char(:2, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), '0000-00-00', to_date(NULL), :2) where user#=:1

_sql_text_map.2h7u1gwu558r7: INSERT INTO PROCESSSKU ( PROCESSID, ITEM, LOC, ITEMLEVEL, SKULEVEL, NETCHGSW, BATCHNUM ) VALUES ( :B4 , :B1 , :B2 , 0, 0, 1, :B3 )
_sql_text_map.2qjq3d3u54ajq: SELECT 1 FROM GV$SESSION A WHERE SID = :B3 AND SERIAL# = :B2 AND INST_ID = :B1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM MANUGISTICS_SCHEMA B WHERE A.USERNAME = B.SCHEMA_NAME AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYS' AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME <> 'JDA_SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME IS NOT NULL)
_sql_text_map.4pp47nzdd67qr: SELECT /* SafetyStockMaxPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC
_sql_text_map.4qguf81v4r5mx: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LOCKERS_NODE_ID = :B4 , STOP_REQUEST_DATE = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP), NODE_STATE = :B3 WHERE NODE_STATE = :B2 AND ROWNUM = 1 AND SRE_NODE.CURRENT_JOB_ID = :B1 RETURNING NODE_ID INTO :O0
_sql_text_map.5dqz0hqtp9fru: select /*+ connect_by_filtering index(sysauth$ i_sysauth1) */ privilege#, bitand(nvl(option$, 0), 72), grantee#, level from sysauth$ connect by grantee#=prior privilege# and privilege#>0 start with grantee#=:1 and privilege#>0
_sql_text_map.6jb71hzd26m1u: SELECT /* SafetyStockPresentationTVQHint */ sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc, sspresentation.displayqty, sspresentation.dmdgroup, sspresentation.presentationqty FROM PROCESSSKU, SSPRESENTATION WHERE ( SSPRESENTATION.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND SSPRESENTATION.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY sspresentation.item, sspresentation.loc, sspresentation.maxss, sspresentation.eff, sspresentation.disc ASC
_sql_text_map.7j17vjtnkzn98: SELECT /* TimePhasedProfileRetrievalHint */ DISTINCT spp.stockoutlvl as stockOutLevel, spp.item as item, spp.loc as loc FROM processsku, SKUPlanningParam spp WHERE processsku.item = spp.item and processsku.loc = spp.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3
_sql_text_map.865qwpcdyggkk: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1
_sql_text_map.9huj1akp5b757: SELECT /*+ ORDERED USE_NL(PROCESSSKU SKU) INDEX (PROCESSSKU PROCESSSKU_BATCH) INDEX (SKU SKU_PK) INDEX (LOC LOC_PK) */ /* SKUBatchRetrievalHint */ sku.item item, sku.loc loc, replentype , (case when sku.ohpost > to_date('01/02/1970', 'mm/dd/yyyy') then sku.ohpost else loc.ohpost end) trueohpost FROM processsku, SKU sku, LOC loc WHERE sku.item = processsku.item and sku.loc = processsku.loc AND processsku.loc = loc.loc and processsku.processID = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2
_sql_text_map.9zg9qd9bm4spu: update user$ set spare6=DECODE(to_char(:2, 'YYYY-MM-DD'), '0000-00-00', to_date(NULL), :2) where user#=:1
_sql_text_map.a6tydqr67a7nu: SELECT SID, SERIAL#, SYS_CONTEXT('userenv', 'instance') FROM V$SESSION WHERE USERNAME = UPPER(:B2 ) AND AUDSID = TO_NUMBER(:B1 )

_sql_text_map.awh9nk7p0r3v0: SELECT /*+ LEADING(PROCESSSKU) USE_NL(vehicleload vehicleloadline) INDEX(processsku PROCESSSKU_PERF) */ /* IntransInTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate, vehicleload.deststatus, vehicleload.sourcestatus, vehicleloadline.actionallowedsw, vehicleloadline.expdate, vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.reviseddate, vehicleloadline.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate ASC

_sql_text_map.cg1ukabw429d9: SELECT /* PlanArrivTVQHint */ planarriv.item, planarriv.dest, planarriv.schedarrivdate, planarriv.expdate, planarriv.actionallowedsw, planarriv.firmplansw, planarriv.qty, planarriv.reviseddate, planarriv.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, PLANARRIV WHERE ( PLANARRIV.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND PLANARRIV.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY planarriv.item, planarriv.dest, planarriv.schedarrivdate, planarriv.expdate ASC
_sql_text_map.cxv29ppvmztwv: SELECT /* IntransInProxyTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE (( vehicleload.deststatus != 3 and vehicleloadline.primaryitem != vehicleloadline.item)) AND ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.batchnum = :2 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate ASC
_sql_text_map.d68fgmq7cuhp7: delete /* SkuStatStaticPurgeHint */ from SKUSTATSTATIC where (item, loc) in (select item, loc from ProcessSku where ProcessID = :1 and batchnum = :2 )
